102.name: Louis IX
102.title: King of France
102.context: Known as Saint Louis, he was a just ruler, a patron of the arts, and a leader of the Seventh and Eighth Crusades.

103.name: Philip IV
103.title: King of France
103.context: Called 'the Fair' for his handsome appearance, he strengthened the monarchy, expelled the Jews, and destroyed the Knights Templar.

104.name: Philip VI
104.title: King of France
104.context: His claim to the throne was contested by Edward III of England, initiating the Hundred Years' War.

105.name: Charles V
105.title: King of France
105.context: Known as 'the Wise', he successfully recovered much of the territory lost to the English in the Hundred Years' War.

106.name: Charles VII
106.title: King of France
106.context: His reign was marked by the appearance of Joan of Arc, who helped lift the Siege of Orléans and turn the tide of the Hundred Years' War.

107.name: Francis I
107.title: King of France
107.context: A great patron of the arts, he initiated the French Renaissance, bringing Leonardo da Vinci to France.

108.name: Henry IV
108.title: King of France and Navarre
108.context: Ended the French Wars of Religion by issuing the Edict of Nantes, granting rights to Protestants. Famously said 'Paris is worth a Mass'.

109.name: Louis XIII
109.title: King of France and Navarre
109.context: Relied heavily on his chief minister, Cardinal Richelieu, to centralize power and crush domestic dissent.

110.name: Louis XIV
110.title: King of France and Navarre
110.context: The 'Sun King', his 72-year reign is the longest of any monarch in European history. He built the Palace of Versailles.

111.name: Louis XV
111.title: King of France and Navarre
111.context: His reign saw France lose its North American colonies in the Seven Years' War, contributing to the decline of royal authority.

112.name: Louis XVI
112.title: King of France and Navarre
112.context: The last king before the French Revolution, he was deposed in 1792 and executed by guillotine the following year.

113.name: Napoleon I
113.title: Emperor of the French
113.context: A military genius who rose from the French Revolution to become Emperor, conquering much of Europe before his final defeat at Waterloo.

117.name: Adolphe Thiers
117.title: President of France
117.context: The first president of the French Third Republic, he negotiated the end of the Franco-Prussian War and crushed the Paris Commune.

118.name: Raymond Poincaré
118.title: President of France
118.context: He was President during World War I, providing strong wartime leadership and advocating for a hard line against Germany.

119.name: Charles de Gaulle
119.title: President of France
119.context: Founder of the Fifth Republic and its first president. He granted independence to Algeria and withdrew France from NATO's military command.

120.name: François Mitterrand
120.title: President of France
120.context: The first socialist president of the Fifth Republic and its longest-serving. He abolished the death penalty and oversaw projects like the Louvre Pyramid.

121.name: Jacques Chirac
121.title: President of France
121.context: His presidency saw France's adoption of the Euro. He famously opposed the 2003 invasion of Iraq.

122.name: Emmanuel Macron
122.title: President of France
122.context: France's youngest president, he came to power with a new centrist party, defeating traditional political forces.

123.name: Jules Grévy
123.title: President of France
123.context: The first President of France to complete a full term, he sought to diminish the powers of the presidency in favor of Parliament, defining the role for decades.

124.name: Albert Lebrun
124.title: President of France
124.context: The last president of the Third Republic, his presidency was marked by the rise of Nazism and the fall of France in World War II.

125.name: Vincent Auriol
125.title: President of France
125.context: The first president of the Fourth Republic, he presided over the beginning of post-war reconstruction and the early stages of decolonization.

126.name: Georges Pompidou
126.title: President of France
126.context: Successor to de Gaulle, he pushed for industrialization and modernized Paris, most famously with the construction of the Centre Pompidou.

127.name: Valéry Giscard d'Estaing
127.title: President of France
127.context: A modernizing president, he lowered the voting age to 18, legalized divorce by mutual consent, and promoted European integration.

128.name: Nicolas Sarkozy
128.title: President of France
128.context: Known for his high-energy style, his presidency focused on liberalizing the economy and responding to the 2008 financial crisis.

129.name: François Hollande
129.title: President of France
129.context: His term was marked by economic struggles and several major terrorist attacks in Paris. He was the first incumbent president not to seek re-election since WWII.

130.name: René Coty
130.title: President of France
130.context: The last president of the Fourth Republic, he presided over a period of political instability and the crisis that led to Charles de Gaulle's return to power.

131.name: Paul Deschanel
131.title: President of France
131.context: His presidency lasted less than a year, ending abruptly due to his deteriorating mental health after he famously fell from a presidential train.

132.name: Alexandre Millerand
132.title: President of France
132.context: A former socialist turned conservative, he clashed with Parliament over his desire to increase presidential powers, ultimately forcing his resignation.

133.name: Gaston Doumergue
133.title: President of France
133.context: The first Protestant President of France, his popular presidency coincided with a period of artistic vibrancy and relative prosperity known as the 'Roaring Twenties'.

134.name: Paul Doumer
134.title: President of France
134.context: His presidency was tragically cut short after less than a year when he was assassinated by a mentally unstable Russian émigré at a Parisian book fair.

135.name: Félix Faure
135.title: President of France
135.context: He strengthened Franco-Russian ties and presided over the Dreyfus Affair. His presidency ended suddenly when he died in office under controversial circumstances.

136.name: Émile Loubet
136.title: President of France
136.context: His presidency saw the 1900 Paris Universal Exposition, the separation of church and state in 1905, and the pardon of Alfred Dreyfus.

137.name: Armand Fallières
137.title: President of France
137.context: An unassuming president, he oversaw the expansion of France's colonial empire in North Africa, particularly in Morocco.

201.name: Philip I
201.title: King of the Franks
201.context: His long reign was marked by conflict with William the Conqueror and his excommunication by the Pope over a disputed marriage.

202.name: Louis VI
202.title: King of the Franks
202.context: Known as 'the Fat', he spent much of his reign consolidating royal power by fighting robber barons around Paris.

203.name: Louis VII
203.title: King of the Franks
203.context: His marriage to Eleanor of Aquitaine was annulled, leading to her marrying Henry II of England, a major cause of future conflict.

204.name: Philip II
204.title: King of France
204.context: A key figure of medieval France, he quadrupled the royal domain and turned France into a prosperous and powerful kingdom.

205.name: Louis VIII
205.title: King of France
205.context: Nicknamed 'the Lion', his short reign was notable for his intervention in England and his conquests in southern France.

206.name: Philip III
206.title: King of France
206.context: Called 'the Bold', he inherited the throne in Tunis during the Eighth Crusade and expanded the royal domain.

207.name: Louis X
207.title: King of France and Navarre
207.context: Known as 'the Quarreller', his brief reign was plagued by factional strife among the nobility.

208.name: John I
208.title: King of France and Navarre
208.context: Known as 'the Posthumous', he was king from his birth but lived for only five days, leading to a succession crisis.

209.name: Philip V
209.title: King of France and Navarre
209.context: Called 'the Tall', he became king after the death of his infant nephew John I, establishing the principle that women could not inherit the throne of France.

210.name: Charles IV
210.title: King of France and Navarre
210.context: The last king of the direct Capetian line. His death without a male heir led to the Valois dynasty ascending the throne.

211.name: John II
211.title: King of France
211.context: Known as 'the Good', he was captured by the English at the Battle of Poitiers and died a prisoner in London.

212.name: Charles VI
212.title: King of France
212.context: Known as 'the Mad', his reign was marred by his frequent bouts of psychosis, leading to political chaos and English victories, including Agincourt.

213.name: Louis XI
213.title: King of France
213.context: Called 'the Prudent' or 'the Universal Spider' for his political scheming, he ended the Hundred Years' War and centralized power.

214.name: Charles VIII
214.title: King of France
214.context: His invasion of Italy in 1494 began the Italian Wars, which would dominate European politics for the next 60 years.

215.name: Louis XII
215.title: King of France
215.context: Known as the 'Father of the People', he was a popular king who reformed the legal system and reduced taxes.

216.name: Henry II
216.title: King of France
216.context: An avid hunter, he famously died in a jousting accident during a tournament celebrating a peace treaty.

217.name: Francis II
217.title: King of France
217.context: The sickly first husband of Mary, Queen of Scots, he reigned for only seventeen months before dying from an ear infection.

218.name: Charles IX
218.title: King of France
218.context: His reign was dominated by the French Wars of Religion and the infamous St. Bartholomew's Day massacre of Huguenots.

219.name: Henry III
219.title: King of France and Poland
219.context: The last Valois king, he was assassinated by a fanatical friar, leading to the succession of the Bourbon dynasty.

220.name: Louis-Napoléon Bonaparte
220.title: President of France
220.context: Nephew of Napoleon I, he was the first president of France to be elected by universal suffrage. He established the Second Republic before staging a coup to become Emperor.

221.name: Napoleon III
221.title: Emperor of the French
221.context: As Emperor, he modernized the French economy and oversaw the grand reconstruction of Paris, creating the city we know today. His reign ended with defeat in the Franco-Prussian War.

222.name: Patrice de MacMahon
222.title: President of France
222.context: A monarchist general who became president, his term was defined by a political crisis that ultimately confirmed the power of Parliament over the President in the Third Republic.

223.name: Sadi Carnot
223.title: President of France
223.context: A popular president who oversaw the 1889 World's Fair featuring the new Eiffel Tower. His presidency was cut tragically short when he was assassinated by an anarchist.

224.name: Jean Casimir-Perier
224.title: President of France
224.context: His presidency is the shortest in French history. He resigned after only 205 days, frustrated by the conflicts of the Dreyfus Affair and his lack of political influence.

225.name: Louis XVIII
225.title: King of France and Navarre
225.context: Brother of Louis XVI, he was restored to the throne after Napoleon's abdication, ruling as a constitutional monarch during the Bourbon Restoration.

226.name: Charles X
226.title: King of France and Navarre
226.context: A staunch ultra-royalist whose reactionary policies led to his overthrow in the July Revolution of 1830, ending the main Bourbon line's rule.

227.name: Louis-Philippe I
227.title: King of the French
227.context: Known as the 'Citizen King', his reign (the July Monarchy) was a period of industrial growth but also political unrest, ending with the 1848 Revolution.
